steps.title: Identify the special triangle
steps.content:
Since
$$
\tan(A)=\sqrt{3}
$$
angle $A$ is $60^{\circ}$. Because $C$ is a right angle, triangle $ABC$ is a $30^{\circ}$-$60^{\circ}$-$90^{\circ}$ triangle.

steps.title: Transfer the angles
steps.content: The triangles are similar, with $A$ corresponding to $D$ and $C$ corresponding to $F$. Therefore $D=60^{\circ}$, $F=90^{\circ}$, and $E=30^{\circ}$.

steps.title: Use the side ratio
steps.content: In triangle $DEF$, side $DF$ is opposite the $30^{\circ}$ angle, and side $DE$ is the hypotenuse. In a $30^{\circ}$-$60^{\circ}$-$90^{\circ}$ triangle, the hypotenuse is twice the shorter leg. $$ DE=2(130)=260 $$ The answer is D.
